sections.paragraph:
ἀνυβρίστως adv. fr. ἀνύβριστος (s. ὕβρις; Ath. 32, 3).

① without being insulted /injured (Ps.-Phoc. 157 H. Jos., Ant. 17, 308; adj. Plut., Pel. 282 [9]; PRyl 117, 26 [III a.d.]), but perh. in an act. sense

② without insolence, decorously (Democr. 73; adj. Plut., Sert. 582 [26]) ἀ. ἀγαλλιᾶσθαι Hs 1:6. The context suggests that the Christian need have no fear of acting insolently when behaving in a correct manner that is misconstrued by worldlings.—DELG s.v. ὕβρις.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

  1. fr. = from

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

Ps.-Phoc Ps.-Phoc , I/II a.d.—List 5

  1. H. = FHort, s. W-H.—List 1, beg.

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

adj. adj. = adjective

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

PRyl PRyl = Catalogue of the Greek Papyri in the John Rylands Library—List 4

perh. perh. = perhaps

act. act. = active

Democr Democr , phil., V/IV b.c.—List 5

Hs Hs = Similitudes

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 91
